frameworks.name: continuous vs categorical representations in VWM
frameworks.one_line: Color memory stored as either fine-grained or category-level

frameworks.name: pupillometry as proxy for mental effort
frameworks.one_line: Pupil dilation indexes cognitive effort during VWM maintenance

frameworks.name: limited-capacity visual working memory
frameworks.one_line: VWM capacity is bounded so representation format trades off load

core_question: Do continuous VWM representations require more mental effort than categorical ones, and does load force a switch?
key_assumptions: Pupil size proxies mental effort during VWM maintenance
Ambiguous colors encourage continuous representations; prototypical colors encourage categorical ones
VWM capacity is fixed and limited
manipulations: Delayed estimation task with probed location
key_findings: At load 1, pupil size was larger for ambiguous than prototypical colors with no precision difference
At load 4, pupil size was smaller and precision was substantially reduced for ambiguous colors
Effect of color type on pupil size gradually inverted across increasing memory load
interpretations: Continuous representations are more effortful than categorical ones
Very few continuous representations (perhaps only one) can be maintained simultaneously, forcing categorical fallback at higher load
